docs.title: flet-video
docs.text:
A cross-platform video player for Flet apps.

It is based on the media_kit Flutter package.

docs.title: Platform Support
docs.text:
This package supports the following platforms:

Platform Supported Windows ✅ macOS ✅ Linux ✅ iOS ✅ Android ✅ Web ✅

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
To install the flet-video package and add it to your project dependencies:

uvpippoetry 
uv add flet-video

pip install flet-video  # (1)!
  1. After this, you will have to manually add this package to your requirements.txt or pyproject.toml.
poetry add flet-video

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
main.py
import random

import flet as ft

import flet_video as ftv


def main(page: ft.Page):
    page.theme_mode = ft.ThemeMode.LIGHT
    page.title = "TheEthicalVideo"
    page.window.always_on_top = True
    page.spacing = 20
    page.horizontal_alignment = ft.CrossAxisAlignment.CENTER

    def handle_pause(e):
        video.pause()
        print("Video.pause()")

    def handle_play_or_pause(e):
        video.play_or_pause()
        print("Video.play_or_pause()")

    def handle_play(e):
        video.play()
        print("Video.play()")

    def handle_stop(e):
        video.stop()
        print("Video.stop()")

    def handle_next(e):
        video.next()
        print("Video.next()")

    def handle_previous(e):
        video.previous()
        print("Video.previous()")

    def handle_volume_change(e):
        video.volume = e.control.value
        page.update()
        print(f"Video.volume = {e.control.value}")

    def handle_playback_rate_change(e):
        video.playback_rate = e.control.value
        page.update()
        print(f"Video.playback_rate = {e.control.value}")

    def handle_seek(e):
        video.seek(10000)
        print("Video.seek(10000)")

    def handle_add_media(e):
        video.playlist_add(random.choice(sample_media))
        print("Video.playlist_add(random.choice(sample_media))")

    def handle_remove_media(e):
        r = random.randint(0, len(video.playlist) - 1)
        video.playlist_remove(r)
        print(f"Popped Item at index: {r} (position {r + 1})")

    def handle_jump(e):
        print("Video.jump_to(0)")
        video.jump_to(0)

    sample_media = [
        ftv.VideoMedia(
            "https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28951144/229373720-14d69157-1a56-4a78-a2f4-d7a134d7c3e9.mp4"
        ),
        ftv.VideoMedia(
            "https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28951144/229373718-86ce5e1d-d195-45d5-baa6-ef94041d0b90.mp4"
        ),
        ftv.VideoMedia(
            "https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28951144/229373716-76da0a4e-225a-44e4-9ee7-3e9006dbc3e3.mp4"
        ),
        ftv.VideoMedia(
            "https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28951144/229373695-22f88f13-d18f-4288-9bf1-c3e078d83722.mp4"
        ),
        ftv.VideoMedia(
            "https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/28951144/229373709-603a7a89-2105-4e1b-a5a5-a6c3567c9a59.mp4",
            extras={
                "artist": "Thousand Foot Krutch",
                "album": "The End Is Where We Begin",
            },
            http_headers={
                "Foo": "Bar",
                "Accept": "*/*",
            },
        ),
    ]

    page.add(
        video := ftv.Video(
            expand=True,
            playlist=sample_media[0:2],
            playlist_mode=ftv.PlaylistMode.LOOP,
            fill_color=ft.Colors.BLUE_400,
            aspect_ratio=16 / 9,
            volume=100,
            autoplay=False,
            filter_quality=ft.FilterQuality.HIGH,
            muted=False,
            on_loaded=lambda e: print("Video loaded successfully!"),
            on_enter_fullscreen=lambda e: print("Video entered fullscreen!"),
            on_exit_fullscreen=lambda e: print("Video exited fullscreen!"),
        ),
        ft.Row(
            wrap=True,
            alignment=ft.MainAxisAlignment.CENTER,
            controls=[
                ft.ElevatedButton("Play", on_click=handle_play),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Pause", on_click=handle_pause),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Play Or Pause", on_click=handle_play_or_pause),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Stop", on_click=handle_stop),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Next", on_click=handle_next),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Previous", on_click=handle_previous),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Seek s=10", on_click=handle_seek),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Jump to first Media", on_click=handle_jump),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Add Random Media", on_click=handle_add_media),
                ft.ElevatedButton("Remove Random Media", on_click=handle_remove_media),
            ],
        ),
        ft.Slider(
            min=0,
            value=100,
            max=100,
            label="Volume = {value}%",
            divisions=10,
            width=400,
            on_change=handle_volume_change,
        ),
        ft.Slider(
            min=1,
            value=1,
            max=3,
            label="PlaybackRate = {value}X",
            divisions=6,
            width=400,
            on_change=handle_playback_rate_change,
        ),
    )


ft.app(main)

docs.title: Changelog
docs.text:
All notable changes to this project will be documented in this file.

The format is based on Keep a Changelog and this project adheres to Semantic Versioning.

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Deployed online documentation: https://flet-dev.github.io/flet-video/
  • Video new property: subtitle_track
  • VideoConfiguration new properties: width, height, scale

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Refactored Video control to use @flet.control dataclass-style definition.
  • Renamed Video event handler properties:
    • on_loaded → on_load
    • on_completed → on_complete
    • on_track_changed → on_track_change
Initial release.

docs.title: Video
docs.text:
Bases: ConstrainedControl

A control that displays a video from a playlist.

RAISES DESCRIPTION AssertionError

If the volume is not between 0.0 and 100.0 (inclusive).

docs.title: alignment: ft.Alignment = field(default_factory=lambda: ft.Alignment.center())
docs.text: Defines the Alignment of the viewport.

docs.title: autoplay: bool = False
docs.text: Whether the video should start playing automatically.

docs.title: configuration: VideoConfiguration = field(default_factory=lambda: VideoConfiguration())
docs.text: Additional configuration for the video player.

docs.title: fill_color: ft.ColorValue = ft.Colors.BLACK
docs.text: Defines the color used to fill the video background.

docs.title: filter_quality: ft.FilterQuality = ft.FilterQuality.LOW
docs.text:
Filter quality of the texture used to render the video output.

Note

Android was reported to show blurry images when using ft.FilterQuality.HIGH. Prefer the usage of ft.FilterQuality.MEDIUM on this platform.

docs.title: fit: ft.BoxFit = ft.BoxFit.CONTAIN
docs.text: The box fit to use for the video.

docs.title: muted: bool = False
docs.text: Defines whether the video player should be started in muted state.

docs.title: on_complete: ft.OptionalControlEventHandler[Video] = None
docs.text: Fires when a video player completes.

docs.title: on_enter_fullscreen: ft.OptionalControlEventHandler[Video] = None
docs.text: Fires when the video player enters fullscreen.

docs.title: on_error: ft.OptionalControlEventHandler[Video] = None
docs.text:
Fires when an error occurs.

Event handler argument's data property contains information about the error.

docs.title: on_exit_fullscreen: ft.OptionalControlEventHandler[Video] = None
docs.text: Fires when the video player exits fullscreen

docs.title: on_load: ft.OptionalControlEventHandler[Video] = None
docs.text: Fires when the video player is initialized and ready for playback.

docs.title: on_track_change: ft.OptionalControlEventHandler[Video] = None
docs.text:
Fires when a video track changes.

Event handler argument's data property contains the index of the new track.

docs.title: pause_upon_entering_background_mode: bool = True
docs.text: Whether to pause the video when application enters background mode.

docs.title: pitch: ft.Number = 1.0
docs.text: Defines the relative pitch of the video player.

docs.title: playback_rate: ft.Number = 1.0
docs.text: Defines the playback rate of the video player.

docs.title: playlist: list[VideoMedia] = field(default_factory=list)
docs.text: A list of VideoMedias representing the video files to be played.

docs.title: playlist_mode: PlaylistMode | None = None
docs.text: Represents the mode of playback for the playlist.

docs.title: resume_upon_entering_foreground_mode: bool = False
docs.text: Whether to resume the video when application enters foreground mode. Has effect only if pause_upon_entering_background_mode is also set to True.

docs.title: show_controls: bool = True
docs.text: Whether to show the video player controls.

docs.title: shuffle_playlist: bool = False
docs.text: Defines whether the playlist should be shuffled.

docs.title: subtitle_configuration: VideoSubtitleConfiguration = field(default_factory=lambda: VideoSubtitleConfiguration())
docs.text: Defines the subtitle configuration for the video player.

docs.title: subtitle_track: VideoSubtitleTrack | None = None
docs.text: Defines the subtitle track for the video player.

docs.title: title: str = 'flet-video'
docs.text: Defines the name of the underlying window & process for native backend. This is visible inside the windows' volume mixer.

docs.title: volume: ft.Number = 100.0
docs.text:
Defines the volume of the video player.

Note

It's value ranges between 0.0 to 100.0 (inclusive), where 0.0 is muted and 100.0 is the maximum volume. An exception will be raised if the value is outside this range.

docs.title: wakelock: bool = True
docs.text: Whether to acquire wake lock while playing the video. When True, device's display will not go to standby/sleep while the video is playing.

docs.title: get_current_position_async() -> ft.Duration
docs.text:
RETURNS DESCRIPTION Duration

The current position of the currently playing media.

docs.title: get_duration_async() -> ft.Duration
docs.text:
RETURNS DESCRIPTION Duration

The duration of the currently playing media.

docs.title: is_completed_async() -> bool
docs.text:
RETURNS DESCRIPTION bool

True if video player has reached the end of

bool

the currently playing media, False otherwise.

docs.title: is_playing_async() -> bool
docs.text:
RETURNS DESCRIPTION bool

True if the video player is currently playing, False otherwise.

docs.title: jump_to(media_index: int)
docs.text: Jumps to the VideoMedia at the specified media_index in the playlist.

docs.title: jump_to_async(media_index: int)
docs.text: Jumps to the VideoMedia at the specified media_index in the playlist.

docs.title: next()
docs.text: Jumps to the next VideoMedia in the playlist.

docs.title: next_async()
docs.text: Jumps to the next VideoMedia in the playlist.

docs.title: pause()
docs.text: Pauses the video player.

docs.title: pause_async()
docs.text: Pauses the video player.

docs.title: play()
docs.text: Starts playing the video.

docs.title: play_async()
docs.text: Starts playing the video.

docs.title: play_or_pause()
docs.text: Cycles between play and pause states of the video player, i.e., plays if paused and pauses if playing.

docs.title: play_or_pause_async()
docs.text: Cycles between play and pause states of the video player, i.e., plays if paused and pauses if playing.

docs.title: playlist_add(media: VideoMedia)
docs.text: Appends/Adds the provided media to the playlist.

docs.title: playlist_add_async(media: VideoMedia)
docs.text: Appends/Adds the provided media to the playlist.

docs.title: playlist_remove(media_index: int)
docs.text: Removes the provided media from the playlist.

docs.title: playlist_remove_async(media_index: int)
docs.text: Removes the provided media from the playlist.

docs.title: previous()
docs.text: Jumps to the previous VideoMedia in the playlist.

docs.title: previous_async()
docs.text: Jumps to the previous VideoMedia in the playlist.

docs.title: seek(position: ft.DurationValue)
docs.text: Seeks the currently playing VideoMedia from the playlist at the specified position.

docs.title: seek_async(position: ft.DurationValue)
docs.text: Seeks the currently playing VideoMedia from the playlist at the specified position.

docs.title: stop()
docs.text: Stops the video player.

docs.title: stop_async()
docs.text: Stops the video player.

docs.title: PlaylistMode
docs.text:
Bases: Enum

Defines the playback mode for the video playlist.

docs.title: LOOP = 'loop'
docs.text: Loop over the playlist & restart it from beginning once end is reached.

docs.title: NONE = 'none'
docs.text: End playback once end of the playlist is reached.

docs.title: SINGLE = 'single'
docs.text: Indefinitely loop over the currently playing file in the playlist.

docs.title: VideoConfiguration
docs.text: Additional configuration for video playback.

docs.title: enable_hardware_acceleration: bool = True
docs.text: Whether to enable hardware acceleration. When disabled, may cause battery drain, device heating, and high CPU usage.

docs.title: hardware_decoding_api: str | None = None
docs.text:
Sets the --hwdec property on native backend.

The default value is platform dependent
  • Windows, GNU/Linux, macOS & iOS : "auto"
  • Android: "auto-safe"

docs.title: height: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text: The fixed height for the video output.

docs.title: output_driver: str | None = None
docs.text:
Sets the --vo property on native backend.

The default value is platform dependent
  • Windows, GNU/Linux, macOS & iOS : "libmpv"
  • Android: "gpu"

docs.title: scale: ft.Number = 1.0
docs.text: The scale for the video output. Specifying this option will cause width & height to be ignored.

docs.title: width: ft.Number | None = None
docs.text: The fixed width for the video output.

docs.title: VideoMedia
docs.text: Represents a media resource for video playback.

docs.title: extras: dict[str, str] | None = None
docs.text: Additional metadata for the media resource.

docs.title: http_headers: dict[str, str] | None = None
docs.text: HTTP headers to be used for the media resource.

docs.title: resource: str
docs.text: URI of the media resource.

docs.title: VideoSubtitleConfiguration
docs.text: Represents the configuration for video subtitles.

docs.title: padding: ft.PaddingValue = field(default_factory=lambda: ft.Padding(left=16.0, top=0.0, right=16.0, bottom=24.0))
docs.text: The padding to be used for the subtitles.

docs.title: text_align: ft.TextAlign = ft.TextAlign.CENTER
docs.text: The text alignment to be used for the subtitles.

docs.title: text_scale_factor: ft.Number = 1.0
docs.text: Defines the scale factor for the subtitle text.

docs.title: text_style: ft.TextStyle = field(default_factory=lambda: ft.TextStyle(height=1.4, size=32.0, letter_spacing=0.0, word_spacing=0.0, color=ft.Colors.WHITE, weight=ft.FontWeight.NORMAL, bgcolor=ft.Colors.BLACK54))
docs.text: The text style to be used for the subtitles.

docs.title: visible: bool = True
docs.text: Whether the subtitles should be visible or not.

docs.title: VideoSubtitleTrack
docs.text: Represents a subtitle track for a video.

docs.title: language: str | None = None
docs.text: The language of the subtitle track, e.g. 'en'.

docs.title: src: str
docs.text:
The subtitle source.

Supported values
  • A URL (e.g. "https://example.com/subs.srt" or "www.example.com/sub.vtt")
  • An absolute local file path (not supported on the web platform)
  • A raw subtitle text string (e.g. the full contents of an SRT/VTT file)

docs.title: title: str | None = None
docs.text: The title of the subtitle track, e.g. 'English'.

docs.title: auto() -> VideoSubtitleTrack
docs.text: Default subtitle track. Selects the first subtitle track.

docs.title: none() -> VideoSubtitleTrack
docs.text: No subtitle track. Disables subtitle output.
